title: Look, ye saints, the sight is glorious
firstLine: Look, ye saints, the sight is glorious;
publicDomainBasis: Thomas Kelly (1769–1855), Joachim Neander (1650–1680). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
Verse 2
sections.text:
Crown the Savior! Angels, crown Him!
Rich the trophies Jesus brings;
In the seat of pow’r enthrone Him,
While the vault of heaven rings:
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Crown the Savior King of kings.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Sinners in derision crowned Him,
Mocking thus the Savior’s claim;
Saints and angels crowd around Him,
Own His title, praise His name:
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Spread abroad the Victor’s fame.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Hark! those bursts of acclamation!
Hark! those loud triumphant chords!
Jesus takes the highest station;
O what joy the sight affords!
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Crown Him! Crown Him!
King of kings, and Lord of lords!
